scope.summary: C4 is now an explicit R3 browser-lane expected-red fixture: strict Chromium pairing plus authenticated action POST is enforced without fallback, while the default lane records the current pre-A5 failure and still proves action POST 200/SSE resume through direct same-origin bootstrap.
out_of_scope: A5 dashboard fetch-first/product behavior is not changed here; the real Chromium pairing path still returns 403 from Origin:null until A5 lands.
Pane 5's A1e VPD/RLS work in catalog_resolver.rs/doctor.rs is untouched.
No workspace-wide cargo gate is claimed from the shared dirty checkout.

entry_points.name: D4 privilege-matrix provisioning
entry_points.trace: `timeout 180 bash scripts/rig/oracle_l1.sh privilege-matrix --log` exited 0 and verified the free23 D4 fixture with ORACLEMCP_D4_CATALOG_OBJECT_ID=126296.

entry_points.name: D4 no-flashback and catalog-blind live lane
entry_points.trace: `timeout 240 env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target ORACLEMCP_D4_* cargo test -p oraclemcp-db --features live-xe --test privilege_matrix_live -- --nocapture` exited 0 in the clean cod6-c4 worktree: d4_no_flashback_principal_gets_typed_refusal_and_connection_stays_usable and d4_catalog_blind_principal_cannot_prove_the_known_guarded_table_read_only both passed, with 0 ignored.

entry_points.name: C4 browser fixture discovery
entry_points.trace: `NODE_PATH=/home/durakovic/projects/oraclemcp/web/node_modules /home/durakovic/projects/oraclemcp/web/node_modules/.bin/playwright test --config web/playwright.live-serve.config.ts --list` exited 0 in the clean cod6-c4 worktree and listed 2 Chromium tests, including `C4: Chromium pairing and authenticated action POST are enforced`.

entry_points.name: C4 expected-red default browser lane
entry_points.trace: `timeout 240 env NODE_PATH=/home/durakovic/projects/oraclemcp/web/node_modules bash scripts/rig/rig_browser_lane.sh run --log` exited 0 in the clean cod6-c4 worktree: omcpb built oraclemcp, Playwright ran 2 Chromium tests, the C4 enforced browser pair+action test was expected-red, and the lane recorded browser pairing 403 from Origin:null plus direct-bootstrap action POST 200 and EventSource resume.

entry_points.name: C4 strict green-mode red proof
entry_points.trace: `timeout 240 env NODE_PATH=/home/durakovic/projects/oraclemcp/web/node_modules OMCP_BROWSER_LANE_EXPECT_PRE_A5_FAILURE=0 bash scripts/rig/rig_browser_lane.sh run --log` exited 1 as expected before A5: both browser-flow tests failed at `pairing form POST should redirect after setting the session`, receiving 403 with body `Forbidden: Origin header is not allowed` instead of 303.

entry_points.name: Scoped formatting and TypeScript checks
entry_points.trace: `cargo fmt --package oraclemcp-db --check`, `bash -n scripts/rig/rig_browser_lane.sh`, and `npm --prefix web exec -- tsc -p web/tsconfig.json --noEmit` all exited 0 in the shared checkout.

limitations: C4 closes on the committed failing fixture per the bead convention; A5 owns the product fix and the green flip.

The strict browser-pairing path is still red before A5 because the real browser POST serializes Origin:null under the current pairing page policy, and the server correctly refuses it instead of accepting literal Origin:null.
The authenticated action POST proof in default mode runs after a direct same-origin pairing bootstrap installs the dashboard cookie, isolating the action route from the known pairing blocker.

known_defects.description: A5 must make Chromium pairing return POST -> 303 -> / without accepting literal Origin:null or weakening dashboard same-origin checks.
